data.title: Clarus Corp’s Exclusive Forum Bylaw Concentrates Shareholder Lawsuits in Delaware, Raising Legal and Governance Risk
data.description:
Clarus Corp (CLAR) has disclosed a new risk, in the Share Price & Shareholder Rights category.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Clarus Corp’s exclusive forum by-law channels most stockholder disputes to the Delaware Court of Chancery or Delaware federal court, potentially limiting investors’ ability to choose alternative venues perceived as more favorable. This concentration of jurisdiction may deter certain claims against directors and officers, which could reduce legal recourse options and affect shareholder confidence.

While the provision aims to create consistency in the application of Delaware law, its enforceability remains subject to judicial scrutiny and does not cover claims under the Exchange Act, which remain in federal courts. The residual uncertainty around enforceability and the narrowed venue choices introduce legal and governance risk that investors must consider when assessing Clarus Corp’s overall risk profile.

Overall, Wall Street has a Moderate Buy consensus rating on CLAR stock based on 1 Buy and 2 Holds.

To learn more about Clarus Corp’s risk factors, click here.

Atyr Pharma Inc. faces a significant listing risk after receiving a Nasdaq deficiency notice on December 4, 2025, for its common stock trading below the $1.00 minimum bid price for 30 consecutive business days.
